title: Mid session 2901 T224 D
body:
NAME OF CANDIDATE: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
                               STUDENT NUMBER: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .



      THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW SOUTH WALES

      SCHOOL OF MATHEMATICS AND STATISTICS


                                 Term 2, 2024


                              MATH2901
              Higher Theory of Statistics
                  Mid-session test D




 (1) TIME ALLOWED – 50 minutes

 (2) TOTAL NUMBER OF QUESTIONS – 3

 (3) ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS

 (4) THE QUESTIONS ARE OF EQUAL VALUE

 (5) THIS PAPER MAY NOT BE RETAINED BY THE CANDIDATE

 (6) Write your answers on the test paper in space provided.
     You may use your own calculators.
     You are allowed a one-sided (A4) handwritten formula sheet of your own. Ask
     if you need more paper.




All answers must be written in ink. Except where they are expressly required pencils
may only be used for drawing, sketching or graphical work.
 Term 2, 2024                          MATH2901                               Page 2


 1. [10 marks] Let X1 and X2 be two independent random variables with respec-
    tive distributions P(λ1 ) and P(λ2 ), λ1 , λ2 > 0, and let S = X1 + X2 .

      a) [2 marks] If λ1 = λ2 , find the conditional expectation E(X1 |S = s), for
         s = 0, 1, . . ..
         (Hint: you may use symmetry to obtain the answer without computation)




      b) [6 marks] In the general case (λ1 not necessarily equal to λ2 ), determine
         the conditional distribution of X1 given S = s, for s = 0, 1, . . ..
         (Hint: It was shown in class that S ∼ P(λ1 + λ2 ) in this setting)




      c) [2 marks] What is E(X1 |S = s) for s = 0, 1, . . .?




                                                               Please see over . . .
 Term 2, 2024                              MATH2901                                 Page 3


 2. [10 marks] Let (X, Y ) be a continuous bivariate vector with joint density

                                fX1 X2 (x1 , x2 ) = c1I{x2 +y2 ≤1}

     for some constant c > 0.

      a) [2 marks] Determine the normalisation constant c.
         (Hint: The area of the disk of radius r > 0 is πr2 .)




      b) [2 marks] Are X1 and X2 independent? Justify.




      c) [3 marks] Find the marginal density of X2 . Deduce its expectation
         E(X2 ).




                                                                     Please see over . . .
 Term 2, 2024                           MATH2901                                Page 4


      d) [3 marks] Find the conditional density fX1 |X2 (x1 |x2 ). Deduce the distri-
         bution of X1 given X2 = x2 , and the conditional expectation E(X1 |X2 =
         x2 ).




                                                                 Please see over . . .
 Term 2, 2024                         MATH2901                             Page 5


 3. Studies have determined that the expected length of the gestation period
    (‘pregnancy’, time between conception and giving birth) for humans is 266
    days, with a standard deviation of 16 days.

      a) [6 marks] Suppose that n = 50 pregnant women are put on a special
         diet just before they become pregnant. Assuming that the diet has no
         effect, provide an approximation for the probability that the average of
         their respective lengths of pregnancy is less than 260 days.
         (Hint: Φ(1.645) = 0.95, Φ(1.96) = 0.975, Φ(2.5758) = 0.995, Φ(2.6517) =
         0.9960)




      b) [2 marks] Do you think that the previous approximation is accurate?
         Explain briefly.




      c) [2 marks] Does it appear that the diet has an effect on the length of
         pregnancy, and should the medical supervisors be concerned? Explain.
